project.name: vim-rogue
project.description: A roguelike dungeon game teaching Vim motions through gameplay. Players navigate 80×40 dungeons across 4 levels using 13 Vim commands (h/j/k/l/w/b/0/$/G/gg/f/t/dd) while avoiding FOV-aware enemies. Features fog-of-war exploration, checkpoint system, motion mastery tracking, and combat mechanics.

nodes.name: Player Movement
nodes.summary: Handles all player navigation through the dungeon using 13 Vim motion commands. Manages position updates, boundary validation, motion tracking, and teaching progression (discovered motions, motion count). Includes two-phase input handling for pending commands.

businessRules: 13 Vim motions: h/j/k/l (cardinal), w/b (word jumps), 0/$ (line ends), G/gg (vertical scan), f/t (find/till), dd (destroy obstacle)
Two-phase input for f/t/dd/gg requires second keypress
Motion cannot pass through walls (except dd destroys obstacles)
Each discovered motion increments motion_count and adds to discovered_motions set
Player position updates validate bounds and passability
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers enemy turn after player motion completes
Updates visibility system after position change
Activates checkpoints when stepping on torchlight tiles
Checks exit condition for level transitions
Processes collision with enemies during movement

nodes.name: Enemy AI
nodes.summary: Manages enemy intelligence including FOV-aware chase behavior using BFS pathfinding, patrol movement within designated areas, collision detection with player, and turn-based orchestration. Enemies respond dynamically to player visibility and position.

businessRules: FOV-aware: chase player only when visible (line-of-sight check)
BFS pathfinding finds shortest path to player
Patrol movement when player not visible, within assigned PatrolArea
Collision with player causes damage and death (lose 1 life, 3 lives total)
Level 4 enemies have 30 HP, vulnerable to melee attacks
Stunned state prevents movement for N turns
Enemies pushed off overlapping positions to avoid stacking
crossDomainInteractions: World aggregate orchestrates all enemy turns each tick
Checks player position and visibility for chase decisions
Processes collisions with player state (HP, lives, death)
Movement animations coordinated with player turn completion

nodes.name: Dungeon Exploration
nodes.summary: Manages the 80×40 grid map with 4 procedural dungeon levels, 5 thematic zones, corridor carving, obstacle placement, enemy spawn points, patrol areas, and torchlight checkpoints. Provides the spatial foundation for all gameplay.

businessRules: 80×40 grid with row-major indexing (grid[y][x])
4 levels with increasing complexity: basic, inverted maze, zigzag, fortress rooms
5 zones with distinct visual themes (color-coded walls)
Tile types: Wall (blocks), Floor (passable), Exit (level transition), Obstacle (dd destroys), Torchlight (checkpoint)
Reachability guaranteed from start to exit on each level
Corridor connectivity between rooms
crossDomainInteractions: Provides spatial data for enemy AI pathfinding
Supplies terrain data for visibility/FOV calculations
Stores patrol area definitions for enemy behavior
Positions torchlight checkpoints for player progression
Validates player movement passability

nodes.name: Visibility and Fog of War
nodes.summary: Computes field of view using ray-casting algorithm, managing three visibility states (Hidden/Visible/Explored) that create the classic fog-of-war effect. Supports multi-source FOV from player position and activated torchlights.

businessRules: FOV_RADIUS defines player visibility range
Three states: Hidden (unseen), Visible (in FOV), Explored (seen before, persist)
Ray-casting algorithm stops at walls
Explored areas persist across visibility updates
Torchlight activation provides permanent FOV radius (TORCHLIGHT_FOV_RADIUS)
Multi-source FOV combines player + activated torchlights
crossDomainInteractions: Informs enemy AI about player visibility for chase decisions
Renders fog overlay in presentation layer
Updates on every player position change
Checkpoint system activates torchlight FOV sources

nodes.name: Game Progression
nodes.summary: Manages game state machine transitions including level completion (reaching exit), death handling (losing lives), checkpoint activation (torchlight), victory condition (completing all 4 levels), and retry mechanics. Tracks session timing and motion mastery.

businessRules: 4 levels total, reaching exit triggers level transition
3 lives per level, enemy collision = -1 life
0 lives → Lost state, option to retry level
Completing level 4 → Won state (victory)
Torchlight activation sets checkpoint (permanent FOV + respawn point)
Death with checkpoint → respawn (restore HP + teleport to checkpoint)
Death without checkpoint → restart level
Motion mastery tracks discovered motions (13 total)
crossDomainInteractions: Advances player level on exit reach
Resets player position on retry/respawn
Updates visibility after level transition
Displays win/lost screens in presentation layer
Tracks elapsed time for session statistics

nodes.name: Execute Vim Motion
nodes.summary: Processes Vim motion input from single-key (h/j/k/l/w/b/0/$/G) and two-phase (f/t/dd/gg) commands, validates input, updates player position, checks boundary conditions, and triggers enemy turn. Core gameplay loop.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Keypress (Vim motion key or ESC/q for pause)

nodes.name: Enemy Turn Orchestration
nodes.summary: After player animation completes, all enemies take turns simultaneously: checking FOV visibility, computing BFS paths, moving toward player or patrolling, processing collisions, and updating animations.

nodes.name: Level Completion
nodes.summary: Triggered when player reaches exit tile: increments level, clears trail, sets new starting position, regenerates map for next level, updates visibility, and checks victory condition (level 4 = win).

nodes.name: Death and Retry
nodes.summary: Handles enemy collision: decrements lives, checks death condition (0 lives), triggers checkpoint respawn or full level restart, updates game state to Lost, and presents retry option in pause menu.

nodes.name: Visibility Update
nodes.summary: Computes FOV from player position and all activated torchlights using ray-casting, demotes visible tiles to explored, marks new visible tiles, and persists explored state across updates.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Player position change or torchlight activation

nodes.name: Checkpoint Activation
nodes.summary: Triggered when player steps on torchlight tile: adds torchlight to permanent FOV sources, updates checkpoint position, enables respawn on death, and grants permanent visibility radius.

nodes.name: Handle Key Input
nodes.summary: Processes keypress through InputState, buffers input for pending commands, delegates single-key motions to execute_motion, and handles pause menu (ESC/q) and melee attack (x).

nodes.name: Dispatch Motion Handler
nodes.summary: PlayerState::handle_motion records the motion, dispatches to specific implementation (step, jump_word_forward/backward, find_char, till_char, delete_obstacle, jump_to_column), and updates tracking.

nodes.name: Validate and Update Position
nodes.summary: Each motion handler checks bounds, verifies passability (can_pass_to), updates position, and returns success status. Special cases: dd deletes obstacles, w/b stop at walls.

nodes.name: Queue Player Animation
nodes.summary: Queues 150ms player movement animation in AnimationState, sets interpolation easing, and blocks input until animation completes.

nodes.name: Trigger Enemy Turn
nodes.summary: After player animation completes, calls enemies_step to orchestrate all enemy movements, collisions, and animations.

nodes.name: Capture Prior Positions
nodes.summary: World::step_enemies captures current positions of all enemies before movement for collision detection and movement result building.

nodes.name: Compute AI Decisions
nodes.summary: Each enemy calls step_toward_player: checks line-of-sight (has_line_of_sight), computes BFS path if visible, or selects patrol movement if hidden. Returns EnemyMovement.

nodes.name: Process Collisions
nodes.summary: Builds EnemyTurn with movement results and collision indices. Game layer processes collisions: decrements player lives, triggers death flow, stuns enemies, and resolves overlapping positions via push_enemies_off_position.

nodes.name: Queue Enemy Animations
nodes.summary: Queues 200ms movement animations for all enemies that moved, with attack effects for combat. Updates AnimationState with interpolated transitions.

nodes.name: Check Exit Condition
nodes.summary: After player motion, checks if player position == exit tile. If yes, triggers level completion flow.

nodes.name: Advance Player Level
nodes.summary: PlayerState::advance_level increments level counter, clears trail, and resets starting position for next level.

nodes.name: Generate New Map
nodes.summary: Builds next level's map using procedural generation (build_level_2/3/4), carving corridors, placing enemies, patrol areas, and torchlights.

nodes.name: Update Visibility
nodes.summary: World::update_visibility computes FOV for new level, marking visible tiles from player start position and any activated torchlights.

nodes.name: Check Victory Condition
nodes.summary: If completed level == 4, sets Session state to Won, displays victory screen with final time, and ends game.

nodes.name: Detect Enemy Collision
nodes.summary: During enemy turn processing, identifies enemies at player position. Triggers damage and death flow.

nodes.name: Decrement Lives
nodes.summary: Player loses 1 life (3 total). If lives > 0, applies temporary invulnerability stun to enemy. If lives == 0, triggers death flow.

nodes.name: Check Checkpoint Active
nodes.summary: Determines if torchlight checkpoint has been activated. If yes, respawn flow. If no, full level restart.

nodes.name: Respawn or Restart Level
nodes.summary: With checkpoint: restore HP, teleport to checkpoint position, maintain torchlight FOV. Without checkpoint: PlayerState::retry_level resets to start, clears trail, regenerates map.

nodes.name: Set Lost State
nodes.summary: Updates Session state to Lost, displays death screen with retry prompt in pause menu. Player can retry level or quit.

nodes.name: Demote Visible to Explored
nodes.summary: World::update_visibility first demotes all currently visible tiles to explored state, persisting fog-of-war memory.

nodes.name: Compute Player FOV
nodes.summary: VisibilityMap::compute_fov casts rays in all directions from player position using ray-casting algorithm, marking visible tiles within FOV_RADIUS.

nodes.name: Compute Torchlight FOV
nodes.summary: For each activated torchlight, calls compute_fov with TORCHLIGHT_FOV_RADIUS, combining multi-source visibility for checkpoint system.

nodes.name: Detect Torchlight Tile
nodes.summary: After player motion, checks if current tile is Tile::Torchlight. If yes, triggers checkpoint activation.

nodes.name: Add Permanent FOV Source
nodes.summary: Adds torchlight position to World's activated torchlights set, making it a permanent FOV source for multi-source visibility.

nodes.name: Set Checkpoint Position
nodes.summary: PlayerState stores torchlight position as checkpoint, enabling respawn on death. Checkpoint persists until new torchlight activated.

nodes.name: Update Visibility with Torchlight
nodes.summary: Triggers visibility update to incorporate new permanent FOV source, expanding visible area around checkpoint.
